title: So Its Come To This A Simpsons Clip Show

jokes.text: Ha, ha! April Fools'. I've been keeping that carton of milk next to the furnace for six weeks. Sucker!
jokes.setup: Homer pretends to be blind from milk, then reveals it's a prank
jokes.punchline: The absurd preparation of keeping milk by the furnace for six weeks
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer - overly elaborate preparation for a simple prank shows his character perfectly. The six weeks detail makes it funnier.

jokes.text: You couldn't fool your mother on the foolingest day of your life... if you had an electrified fooling machine.
jokes.setup: Bart challenges Homer to a prank war
jokes.punchline: Homer's absurdly elaborate insult with made-up words and escalating imagery
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating insult with invented superlatives. 'Foolingest' and 'electrified fooling machine' are pure comedy gold. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: God bless those pagans.
jokes.setup: Lisa explains April Fools' pagan origins
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual blessing of pagans shows his obliviousness to religious contradiction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - completely missing the religious irony. Economy of words with maximum character insight.

jokes.text: Blood for Baal! Blood for Baal!
jokes.setup: Lisa explains pagan New Year rituals
jokes.punchline: Cartoonish depiction of ancient ritual with chanting
jokes.explanation: Standard historical parody. Works as setup for the larger April Fools' origin story but not particularly clever on its own.

jokes.text: New Year's was three months ago, but here's a present anyhoo. It's ram's blood for your godless ceremony.
jokes.setup: Ned appears to be giving a friendly gift to pagans
jokes.punchline: The 'gift' is ram's blood delivered with Ned's characteristic passive-aggressive politeness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned Flanders - turning cruelty into neighborly politeness. The contrast between tone and content is expertly done.

jokes.text: Ah, beer, my one weakness. My Achilles' heel, if you will.
jokes.setup: Homer contemplates Bart's weakness to exploit
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately identifies his own obvious weakness instead
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer self-awareness without wisdom. The formal 'if you will' makes his obvious weakness sound scholarly.

jokes.text: Beer. Beer is the cheese. But how to use it?
jokes.setup: Homer plots how to use beer as bait
jokes.punchline: Mixing metaphors by calling beer 'the cheese' in a trap context
jokes.explanation: Homer's mixed metaphor shows his confused but determined plotting. Simple but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Whoo! It's a good thing that beer wasn't shaken up any more... or I'd have looked quite the fool, an April fool, as it were.
jokes.setup: Homer opens a beer can, relieved it wasn't shaken
jokes.punchline: His formal language 'as it were' contrasts with the impending disaster we know is coming
jokes.explanation: Sets up the impending explosion while showing Homer's obliviousness. The formal tone makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Wow! I'm in pizza-face paradise! Moron.
jokes.setup: Bart tricks a kid about free acne pads
jokes.punchline: Kid's excited reaction followed by Bart's casual insult
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective Bart prank. The casual cruelty is character-appropriate but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: ¡Ay! No es bueno.
jokes.setup: Apu sees the shaken beer can Homer is about to open
jokes.punchline: His worried Spanish reaction to the impending disaster
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction gag. Apu's multilingual response adds character flavor and builds tension for the explosion.

jokes.text: Excessive alcohol consumption can cause liver damage... and cancer of the rectum. Mmm, beer.
jokes.setup: Serious health warning about alcohol
jokes.punchline: Homer's dreamy 'Mmm, beer' response shows complete disregard for the warning
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment. The timing and contrast between serious warning and Homer's response is expertly executed.

jokes.text: That sounded like an explosion at the old Simpson place. Forget it. That's two blocks away.
jokes.setup: Wiggum hears an explosion at the Simpsons'
jokes.punchline: His lazy dismissal because it's 'two blocks away'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wiggum - incompetent and lazy police work delivered with authority. Shows character priorities brilliantly.

jokes.text: Looks like there's beer coming out of the chimney. I am proceeding on foot. Call in a code eight. We need pretzels.
jokes.setup: Wiggum notices beer coming from chimney
jokes.punchline: He treats it as an emergency requiring pretzels as backup
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from lazy to suddenly professional when beer is involved. 'Code eight' and 'pretzels' is inspired absurdity.

jokes.text: April Fools'. He's very much alive... although I'm afraid he may never walk again.
jokes.setup: Doctor tells Marge that Homer is dead, then reveals it's April Fools'
jokes.punchline: The 'good news' is immediately followed by potentially worse news
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection structure but the April Fools' medical joke feels slightly forced. Still effective dark comedy.

jokes.text: Bart, how could you shock your little sister? My finger slipped-- Ahh! So did mine-- [Yelps] Bart, Lisa, stop that!
jokes.setup: Family is wired to shock machines, Bart shocks Lisa
jokes.punchline: Revenge shocking escalates despite Marge's protests
jokes.explanation: Simple escalation gag. Family dynamics work well but the concept isn't particularly original.

jokes.text: This is the greatest thrill of my life! I'm king of the world!
jokes.setup: Homer falling repeatedly down cliff
jokes.punchline: His joy at the moment before another painful impact
jokes.explanation: Homer's oblivious joy before pain, with Titanic reference adding cultural layer

jokes.text: You're a veterinarian? That's right. And for an extra $20... I'll give Homer a tick bath and then spay him.
jokes.setup: Family realizes their 'doctor' is actually a veterinarian
jokes.punchline: Vet offers additional pet services for Homer
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist premise with solid execution. The casual offer of spaying is particularly funny.

jokes.text: Shop around. You can't beat that price.
jokes.setup: Vet offers to spay Homer for $20
jokes.punchline: Delivers it like a legitimate business pitch
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery. Treating an absurd offer as normal business practice elevates the joke.

jokes.text: It's true. You have good coping skills.
jokes.setup: Homer survives being attacked by forest animals
jokes.punchline: Doctor's clinical understatement of Homer's traumatic experience
jokes.explanation: Solid deadpan reaction to absurd situation. Medical professional's clinical detachment makes it funnier.

jokes.text: No! No! Beer bring pain!
jokes.setup: Moe brings Homer beer in the hospital
jokes.punchline: Homer's caveman-like rejection of his former weakness
jokes.explanation: Good character development moment. The primitive speech pattern emphasizes Homer's trauma and creates humor.

jokes.text: Oh, Marge, what if I wind up as some vegetable watching TV on the couch? My important work will never be completed.
jokes.setup: Homer worries about becoming a vegetable
jokes.punchline: He describes his current lifestyle while calling his 'work' important
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic self-awareness where Homer describes his current life as his nightmare scenario

jokes.text: [Homer thinking] Chocolate. Chocolate.
jokes.setup: Doctor tries to motivate comatose Homer to move
jokes.punchline: Homer's internal monologue shows he's mentally active but only thinking about chocolate
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - conscious but prioritizing food over recovery. Simple but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Can't breathe. Sweet chocolate... cutting off air. Mmm! Nuts.
jokes.setup: Homer choking on chocolate
jokes.punchline: Still appreciating the nuts while being strangled by candy
jokes.explanation: Homer's priorities remain food-focused even while choking to death

jokes.text: Coma? Pfftt. Why, I go in and out of comas all the-- [Snores]
jokes.setup: Grampa dismisses Homer's coma as no big deal
jokes.punchline: Immediately falls into a coma mid-sentence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Grampa joke - his timing and character perfectly executed. The immediate demonstration of his claim is comedy gold.

jokes.text: It's like one of those TV shows where they show a bunch of clips from old episodes.
jokes.setup: Grampa describes what being in a coma is like
jokes.punchline: Describes exactly what this clip show episode is doing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke that acknowledges the clip show format while staying in character. Self-aware without breaking the fourth wall completely.

jokes.text: Hi, everybody! [All] Hi, Dr. Nick.
jokes.setup: Mr. Burns demands a second opinion
jokes.punchline: Dr. Nick's catchphrase entrance with crowd response
jokes.explanation: Classic callback that works because of character establishment. Not original but satisfying for fans.

jokes.text: Just to be safe, we better pull the plug. Yoink!
jokes.setup: Dr. Nick sees no signs of life in Homer
jokes.punchline: Immediately pulls the plug with his characteristic sound effect
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dr. Nick - incompetent but decisive. The 'yoink' sound effect adds to his character's childlike approach to medicine.

jokes.text: I have an unorthodox solution-- a fantastic voyage, if you will. Three scientists-- one a beautiful woman-- will be shrunk to microscopic size. They will then rendezvous in Mr. Simpson's lower colon.
jokes.setup: Dr. Nick proposes an 'unorthodox solution' referencing the movie Fantastic Voyage
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous movie plot applied to Homer's medical case, ending in his colon
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture reference with absurdist twist. Dr. Nick's movie-inspired 'medical' solution fits his character.

jokes.text: There and there. Don't worry. The blood'll hold it right on your face. And that's how we shave.
jokes.setup: Homer teaches Bart to shave by putting toilet paper on cuts
jokes.punchline: His casual acceptance that bleeding is normal, presented as wisdom
jokes.explanation: Good father-son moment that shows Homer's well-meaning incompetence. His matter-of-fact delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Gotcha! It's April Fools' for two more minutes.
jokes.setup: Homer announces they're going to Hawaii after waking from coma
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's an April Fools' joke, bringing the episode full circle
jokes.explanation: Good callback that brings the episode full circle. Homer returning to pranks shows he's recovered his personality.

jokes.text: Dad, it's May 16. You were in that coma for seven weeks.
jokes.setup: Homer claims it's still April Fools'
jokes.punchline: Lisa's matter-of-fact correction reveals Homer's extended coma
jokes.explanation: Lisa's practical correction of Homer's time confusion works well. Simple but effective reality check.

jokes.text: You lost five percent of your brain. Me lose brain? Uh-oh! Why I laugh?
jokes.setup: Doctor informs Homer he lost brain function
jokes.punchline: Homer's speech immediately deteriorates, then he questions his own laughter
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending joke showing immediate consequences. Homer's confusion about his own laughter adds another layer.
